diagrams.title: IP Address Classes Range
diagrams.description: Shows the ranges for Class A, B, C, D, E IP addresses
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for identifying IP address classes and their ranges

diagrams.title: OSI Model and Routing
diagrams.description: Shows how routing functions at different OSI layers
elements: Network Layer (3) - IP, Routing happens here
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which layer routing protocols operate at and their relationship with other network functions

diagrams.title: Routing Decision Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flow of how a router makes forwarding decisions
elements: Router searches routing table for longest prefix match
If match found, forwards to specified next hop
If no match, checks for default route
If no default, packet is dropped
ICMP destination unreachable sent to source
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the packet forwarding logic in routers

diagrams.title: Subnetting Example - Class C
diagrams.description: Visual representation of subnetting a Class C network
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how subnets are created and their address ranges
